title: Palestinians Hold First Gaza Elections in 20 Years; 22.7% Turnout in Deir al-Balah as IDF Strikes During Vote
detail: Palestinians held municipal elections on April 25, 2026 — the first elections in Gaza in nearly 20 years and the first Palestinian vote of any kind since the October 7, 2023 war began. Voting in Gaza was limited to Deir al-Balah (central Gaza), with 12 polling stations and approximately 70,000 eligible voters; turnout was 22.7% amid security concerns and displacement. West Bank turnout reached approximately 53.4%. Most candidates ran under Fatah or as independents; Hamas was formally excluded — candidates required to recognize Israel and support the two-state solution. Hamas permitted its civil police to guard polling stations and called the vote 'an important step.' Voting ended at 6 p.m. in Deir al-Balah due to electricity constraints and at 7 p.m. in the West Bank. Palestinian observers noted the limited scope of the exercise but welcomed the first expression of democratic participation in Gaza in two decades.

media.caption: Gaza holds first municipal elections in 20 years in Deir al-Balah, April 25, 2026

title: IDF Airstrike at Entrance to Maghazi Refugee Camp Kills Three Palestinians
detail: An Israeli airstrike targeted a car at the entrance to the Maghazi refugee camp in central Gaza on April 25, 2026, killing three Palestinians and wounding others. The strike occurred on the same day as Palestinian municipal elections in nearby Deir al-Balah. It adds to the post-October 2025 ceasefire death toll, which has now exceeded 800 Palestinians killed since ceasefire implementation. The IDF has not publicly confirmed the specific strike. Bodies were reported transferred to nearby hospitals. The attack illustrates the continuing pattern of Israeli military operations in central Gaza despite the nominal ceasefire.

media.caption: IDF airstrike kills three Palestinians at Maghazi refugee camp entrance on Palestinian election day, April 25, 2026

title: IDF Strikes Hamas Police Officers Deployed as Election Security in Sheikh Radwan, Gaza City
detail: The Hamas-run interior ministry reported on April 25, 2026 that Israeli forces conducted a strike near a police station in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood of western Gaza City, targeting Hamas police officers deployed for election-day security. The strike took place as Hamas police guarded polling stations in Deir al-Balah and maintained public order in Gaza during the first Palestinian municipal elections in 20 years. The IDF did not publicly confirm the specific strike. Hamas condemned the strike as a deliberate targeting of officers performing civil security duties on election day, arguing it represented an attempt to undermine Palestinian civic processes.

media.caption: IDF strikes Hamas police near Sheikh Radwan police station during Gaza election day, April 25, 2026

title: Israeli Settlers Raid Duma Village, West Bank — Woman and Child Injured
detail: Israeli settlers raided the Palestinian village of Duma in the central West Bank shortly after 7 a.m. on April 25, 2026, wounding a woman and a child. Settlers also attacked an adjacent Bedouin hamlet, reportedly violating a closed military zone order imposed by the IDF. Duma residents threw stones at the settlers; one settler was reportedly lightly wounded. Several Palestinian vehicles were vandalized; a child suffered facial injuries from glass shards. The attack is the latest in a sustained wave of settler violence following the April 21–23 Deir Dibwan and al-Mughayyir settler killings — the deadliest three-day settler violence episode of 2026 — in which four Palestinians were killed by settlers. Duma is internationally known as the village where the Dawabsheh family was burned alive by Israeli settlers in July 2015, killing an 18-month-old baby and both parents.

media.caption: Settler attack on Duma village injures woman and child in the West Bank, April 25, 2026
